name: Learned Helplessness — Uncontrollable Failure
description: Repeated exposure to uncontrollable negative outcomes leads to passivity and failure to act even when control becomes available. The person learns 'nothing I do matters.'

history: proposed 5 improvements over 6 months, all dismissed without consideration
asked for different assignments, denied each time

stimulus.description: New manager arrives and genuinely asks: 'What ideas do you have for improving our process? I want to hear from everyone.'

expected.behavior: says 'I don't really have any ideas' or gives minimal response despite having many ideas

sources: Seligman & Maier 1967 — Failure to Escape Traumatic Shock
Abramson, Seligman & Teasdale 1978 — Learned Helplessness in Humans
